name: Pilot: Airplane
description: Includes old propeller, single and twin engine types.

name: Pilot: Automobile
description: Manual and automatic transmissions; includes dune buggies, jeeps, and small trucks.

name: Pilot: Bicycling
description: The character knows how to ride bicycles and can ride over rough terrain, reach two times normal running speed, travel cross-country at normal running speed, and perform tricks and jumps on a bike. Maximum speed can be maintained for a time equal to P.E. x5 in minutes. The skill also includes basic maintenance and repairs of bicycles.

successOutcomes.summary: Maintains control over rough terrain, cross-country travel, tricks, jumps, and routine bicycle maintenance.

failureResults.summary: A failed roll when performing tricks or jumps means a crash.

performanceFormulas.linkSummary: On rough terrain the character can reach two times normal running speed.
Cross-country travel is at normal running speed.
Maximum speed can be maintained for a time equal to P.E. x5 in minutes.

unquantifiedMechanics.summary: Tricks and jumps require a skill roll at the character's bicycling skill level.
unquantifiedMechanics.appliesWhen: When performing tricks or jumps on a bicycle.

unquantifiedMechanics.summary: Includes basic maintenance and repairs of bicycles.

name: Pilot: Boat: Motor and Hydrofoils
description: These include all types of small motor driven boats and yachts.

name: Pilot: Boat: Sail Type
description: Small sailing and medium sized sailing yachts, and fishing boats.

name: Pilot: Boat: Ships
description: This includes all large sailing and motor driven seafaring vessels.

name: Pilot: Boats: Paddle Types/Canoe/Kayak
description: Includes paddled boats and canoes, rowboats and knee boards, as well as the kayak. Speed in still water is usually equal to the character's P.S. attribute (treated for all purposes as Spd) and can be maintained for P.E. x5 in minutes without pause. The skill also includes basic principles behind using currents, maintenance, and even tricks like sculling and right-siding a flipped canoe.

successOutcomes.summary: Handles paddled craft confidently in still water, uses currents effectively, and performs maintenance or recovery maneuvers such as sculling and righting a flipped canoe.

failureResults.summary: Loses control of the craft, mishandles currents, or fails a trick maneuver such as sculling or righting a flipped canoe.

performanceFormulas.linkSummary: Speed in still water is usually equal to the character's P.S. attribute, treated for all purposes as Spd.
Speed can be maintained for P.E. x5 in minutes without pause.

unquantifiedMechanics.summary: Includes basic principles behind using currents and vessel maintenance.
unquantifiedMechanics.appliesWhen: When paddling in moving water or maintaining paddle craft.

unquantifiedMechanics.summary: Includes tricks such as sculling and right-siding a flipped canoe.

name: Combat Driving
description: This skill supplements other driving skills by helping to make the character a master of the roads when driving automobiles, jeeps, trucks, motorcycles, and other ground vehicles. It helps turn the character into an aggressive road hog and cool-headed "chicken" player. Tricks, dangerous maneuvers, and high-speed car chases are no sweat for this character, although passengers are likely to fear for their lives. This is a supplemental piloting skill applicable only to ground vehicles and does not have a base skill or level of progression other than reducing penalties an additional one point per three levels of the driver's experience.

rollWithImpact.condition: When driving a ground vehicle and rolling with a crash or impact

mechanicProgressions.summary: Penalties for tricks, vehicular attacks (ram, sideswipe, etc.), and evasive maneuvers are reduced by half when this character is at the wheel.
mechanicProgressions.targetLabel: Tricks, vehicular attacks, and evasive maneuver penalties (after halving)

interdependentKnowledge.summary: Supplements other ground-vehicle piloting skills (automobiles, jeeps, trucks, motorcycles, etc.); it is not a standalone vehicle operation skill.

unquantifiedMechanics.summary: Penalties for tricks, vehicular attacks (ram, sideswipe, etc.), and evasive maneuvers are reduced by half when this character is at the wheel.
Can fire a weapon from a moving vehicle at only a -2 penalty.
unquantifiedMechanics.appliesWhen: When firing from a moving ground vehicle.

unquantifiedMechanics.summary: Can make an Aimed or Called Shot at -2 to strike while in a moving vehicle, or talk or engage in some other activity while driving without the usual penalty.
unquantifiedMechanics.appliesWhen: When performing secondary actions while driving a moving ground vehicle.

unquantifiedMechanics.summary: Penalty reductions improve by one additional point per three levels of the driver's experience.
unquantifiedMechanics.appliesWhen: When resolving tricks, vehicular attacks, or evasive maneuver penalties while driving.

narrativeAdvantages.summary: Passengers are likely to fear for their lives during aggressive driving, tricks, and high-speed chases.
narrativeAdvantages.appliesWhen: When others ride with this character during combat driving.

name: Pilot: Helicopter

prerequisites.condition: Required to operate weapons on a combat helicopter.

description: The specialized skill required in flying all types of helicopters, including combat 'copters.

name: Horsemanship

specialization.notes: At the G.M.'s discretion, this skill can also apply to riding supernatural animals/monsters (not applicable to intelligent creatures) at a base skill of 24% +4% per level of experience.

description: In certain places, the horse is a popular means of transportation. The skill includes the basic principles of riding, the care and feeding of the animal, recognizing breed and quality, charging, and jumps. The percentile number is used whenever the character tries to determine breed and quality, and for special jumps or maneuvers.

unquantifiedMechanics.summary: A person cannot shoot a projectile weapon of any kind (bow and arrow or gun) with any degree of accuracy from a moving animal.
unquantifiedMechanics.appliesWhen: Firing projectile weapons from a moving mount.

unquantifiedMechanics.summary: Bursts are possible if the horse is moving quickly, but not at a full gallop.
unquantifiedMechanics.appliesWhen: Firing burst or automatic fire from a mount that is moving quickly but not at a full gallop.

situationalRuleOverrides.summary: Aimed shots are impossible on a moving animal.
situationalRuleOverrides.whenContext: Firing a projectile weapon from a moving animal.

situationalRuleOverrides.summary: At full gallop, the shooter can fire only as wild.
situationalRuleOverrides.whenContext: Firing from a horse at a full gallop.

name: Pilot: Hover Craft (ground)
description: The knowledge needed in piloting hover cycles and other hover vehicles used for ground transport. These vehicles can be tricky, because they utilize several air jets and directional thrusters that pushes the craft along, above the ground, on a cushion of air. The vehicles can usually attain great speeds and are capable of driving over incredibly rugged terrains and make sensational jumps over craters and ravines.

name: Pilot: Jet Aircraft
description: Includes large and small commercial transport jets.

name: Pilot: Jet Fighters
description: Training includes flying, maneuvering, aerial combat strategies, and typical fighter jet weapon systems.

name: Pilot: Jet Packs
description: This is the piloting skill of backpack like units that strap to the back of an individual to carry him airborne. The typical jet pack is a one person unit capable of hovering above the ground and flying at an average altitude of 300 feet (91.5 m).

name: Pilot: Motorcycle
description: This skill includes the piloting of all two and three wheeled vehicles such as motorcycles of all types, dirt bikes, and mopeds.

name: Pilot: Tanks and APCs
description: Military vehicles often have unconventional controls and handle differently than conventional vehicles, especially the many ton, armored, combat vehicles such as tanks and armored personnel carriers (APC). Thus, special training is required to pilot them.

name: Pilot: Tracked & Construction Vehicles
description: Includes landcrawlers, half-tracks, light tanks, and big-wheeled heavy construction vehicles and earthmovers like bulldozers, steam shovels/excavators, scrapers, graders, tractors, loaders, backhoes, dump trucks, haulers, and similar equipment.

successOutcomes.summary: Operates tracked and construction vehicles effectively for their intended role.

failureResults.summary: Mishandles the vehicle, loses control, or fails a difficult maneuver.

situationalRuleOverrides.summary: When piloting tanks and APCs with this skill, the character suffers -1 attack per melee round.
situationalRuleOverrides.whenContext: When piloting tanks or APCs using this skill.

unquantifiedMechanics.summary: Can pilot tanks and APCs but at a -15% penalty and -1 attack per melee round.
unquantifiedMechanics.appliesWhen: When operating tanks or armored personnel carriers.

name: Pilot: Truck
description: This piloting skill specifically applies to large cargo and transport vehicles like eight to sixteen wheeled commercial trucks and multi-ton transports.
